data.title: Madison Square Garden Entertainment repurchases $25 million class A common stock
data.description:
• Madison Square Garden Entertainment (NYSE:MSGE) announced on Friday that the company repurchased 623,271 shares of MSGE Class A common stock at an average price of $40.11 per share for an aggregate purchase price of approximately $25 million from August 20, 2025, through September 11, 2025.
  • The share repurchases were funded using cash on hand, said the company. 
  • Since the company was spun off from Sphere Entertainment in April 2023, the company has repurchased 6,106,239 shares of MSGE Class A common stock for an aggregate purchase price of approximately $205 million.
  • The company has approximately $45 million remaining under its existing share repurchase authorization.
  • MSGE +0.11% premarket to $43.35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Alaska Silver files registration statement for proposed U.S. IPO
data.description:
• Alaska Silver (TSXV:WAM:CA)  has publicly filed a registration statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) with respect to a proposed initial public offering of its securities in the United States.
  • The offering price and certain terms of the securities to be offered will be determined in the context of the market at the time of pricing.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Peraso secures $0.9 million order
data.description:
• Peraso (NASDAQ:PRSO) announced on Friday that it has received an initial volume order totaling approximately $0.9 million for its PRM2141X module from a global equipment supplier to the fixed wireless access ("FWA") market.
  • The company said its PRM2141X module enables fiber-class broadband connectivity and allows original equipment manufacturers ((OEMs)) and service providers to offer cost-efficient and rapidly deployable wireless equipment.
  • "This order from a new OEM customer marks a major milestone for Peraso and further establishes Peraso at the forefront of the 60 GHz FWA equipment market," said Ron Glibbery, CEO of Peraso. 
  • PRSO +4.06% premarket to $1.28.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: TON Strategy announces initial share repurchases under $250 million program
data.description:
• TON Strategy (NASDAQ:TONX) has repurchased over 250,000 shares of its common stock under its previously announced $250 million share repurchase program.
  • The company repurchased shares at an average of $8.32 per share.
  • Under the share repurchase program, launched on September 8, 2025, the company can buy back up to $250 million of its common stock.
  • TONX +2.76% premarket to $8.2
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Giorgio Armani heirs instructed to sell company or go public
data.description:
Fashion titan Giorgio Armani left instructions for his heirs to either sell off his eponymous fashion house to peers including LVHM Moet Hennessy (OTCPK:LVMHF) (OTCPK:LVMUY), L’Oreal S.A. (OTCPK:LRLCF) (OTCPK:LRLCY), or EssilorLuxottica (OTCPK:ESLOF) (OTCPK:ESLOY) or go pursue a public offering.

Armani died on September 4.

Under the temrs of Armani's will, at least 15% of Giorgio Armani S.p.A must be sold within 15 months of his death with a majority sold over the next several years. 

In the event of a sale, the foundation and Dell’Orco would need to agree on a buyer of “equal standing” and those that are linked commercially to the business. Following the initial 15% sale, the subsequent buyer’s holdings would need to be increased to 30% and 54.9% in three to five years from Armani’s death.

If Armani’s heirs are unable to find buyers, the will leaves instructions for the company to pursue an IPO on an Italian exchange or an exchange of “comparable status.” Under this scenario, the Giorgio Armani foundation – Fondazione Giorgio Armani – and Armani’s long-time partner Leo Dell’Orco would retain 70% of the public company.

The terms of Armani’s will come as a surprise given Armani’s penchant for privacy and autonomy. He remained the major shareholder in Giorgio Armani S.p.A after founding the fashion house with Sergio Galeotti in 1975.

Giorgio Armani S.p.A reported sales of more than $2B in 2024.

data.title: Investor pushes Sunstone Hotel to sell itself or liquidate assets - Reuters
data.description:
Investor Tarsadia Capital is urging Sunstone Hotel Investments (NYSE:SHO) to sell itself or liquidate its assets. Sunstone rose 2.1% in premarket trading.

Tarsadia, which owns a 3.4% stake in Sunstone Hotel (NYSE:SHO), also wants the real estate investment trust to appoint new directors to its 8-member board, according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited a letter viewed by the publication. If the board decides to do nothing, the investor warned that it may start a proxy battle later this year. 

Tarsadia is putting pressure on the hotel REIT after months of constructive conversations between the investor and Sunstone's management and board, though those talks have hit an impasse, according to the report.

Sunstone didn't immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment.

data.title: Sphere Entertainment repurchases additional $22.5 million class A common stock
data.description:
• Sphere Entertainment (NYSE:SPHR) announced on Friday that the company repurchased 425,219 shares of SPHR Class A common stock at an average price of $52.91 per share for an aggregate purchase price of approximately $22.5 million from September 3, 2025, through September 11, 2025.
  • The company said the share repurchases were funded using cash on hand.
  • Sphere Entertainment has now repurchased 1,054,247 shares of SPHR Class A common stock at an average price of $47.43 per share for an aggregate purchase price of $50 million in 2025 to-date.
  • The company said it will continue to evaluate additional opportunistic share repurchases going forward and has approximately $300 million remaining under its existing share repurchase authorization.
  • SPHR -0.98% premarket to $56.4.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Blue Hat completes $13.3M in gold trades in H1, projects 550kg for full year
data.description:
Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology (NASDAQ:BHAT) announced that it completed over 123KG ($13,326,754) in gold trading during the first half of 2025, and projects that trading volume may potentially reach 550 kg during fiscal year 2025.

"This represents progress in the company's strategic transformation toward gold-focused commodity trading," Chen Xiaodong, CEO of the company, said.

data.title: Global Indemnity declares $0.35 dividend
data.description:
• Global Indemnity (NYSE:GBLI) declares $0.35/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.79%
  • Payable Oct. 6; for shareholders of record Sept. 29; ex-div Sept. 29.
  • See GBLI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Bitdeer reports 33% rise in self-mined bitcoins in August
data.description:
• Bitdeer Technologies (NASDAQ:BTDR) self-mined 375 bitcoins in August, an increase of ~33% from the past month due to higher average self-mining hashrate from energization of SEALMINERs.
  • Self-mining hashrate rose by 35% to 30.0 EH/s on continued deployment of SEALMINERs. "Our operational team continues to execute and we are well on our way to achieving our ambitious target of 40 EH/s of self-mining by the end of October 2025.” said Matt Kong, Chief Business Officer at Bitdeer.
  • Total hash rate under management as of August 31, 2025 across the company’s primary business lines were 44.2 EH/s vs. 36.4 EH/s at the end of July.
  • Source: Press Release
